source: Roslyn analyzers, .NET best practices, OWASP .NET guidelines

rules.name: null-forgiving-operator
rules.fix_hint: Use null checks or nullable reference types.

rules.name: dynamic-execution
rules.message: Dynamic code execution or process spawn — validate input.
rules.fix_hint: Avoid dynamic execution; use parameterized approaches.

rules.name: generic-exception-catch
rules.message: Catching base Exception — catch specific exception types.

rules.name: console-output
rules.message: Console output in production code — use ILogger.
rules.fix_hint: Inject ILogger<T> and use structured logging.

rules.name: hardcoded-credential
rules.fix_hint: Use User Secrets, environment variables, or Azure Key Vault.

rules.name: string-concatenation-in-logging
rules.message: String interpolation/concatenation in logging — use structured log templates.

rules.name: sql-concatenation
rules.message: SQL string concatenation — risk of SQL injection.
rules.fix_hint: Use parameterized queries, EF Core LINQ, or Dapper with parameters.

rules.name: async-void
rules.message: async void method — exceptions cannot be caught by caller.
rules.fix_hint: Return Task instead of void. async void is only acceptable for event handlers.

file_size._note: Lines of code. Controllers and services should be focused; tests and migrations get more room.

boundaries.name: No infrastructure imports in domain layer
boundaries.message: Domain layer must remain infrastructure-free — use interfaces and pure C# types.
